name: LazyPI-Rot6
description: k<m: rotation (k×k eigh) + full PI every 6. k>=m small m: eigh. k>=m large m: full PI every 6.
author: MiMo V2 Pro Free
result: Score 34.7 vs previous best 24.9 (LazyPowerIter-4). Key insight: for k<<m (10x2000 case), use rotation (k×k eigh of Bk^T A Bk) between full PI steps to maintain diagonalization quality. For k>=m (2000x2000), use lazy full PI every 6 steps. For m<=500, full eigh each step is cheap. The rotation step for k<m improves EV from 0.907 to 0.938 at negligible cost (0.22ms). Full PI every 6 gives good EV (0.943/0.938) at ~8.5ms/step amortized.
